lede: A look at August 7 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lauren Hemp and Ryan Lavarnway, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Air India Express Flight 1344
events.desc: Air India Express Flight 1344 overshoots the runway at Calicut International Airport in the Malappuram district of Kerala, India, and crashes, killing 21 of the 190 people on board.

events.title: 1998 United States embassy bombings
events.desc: Bombings at United States embassies in Dar es Salaam, Tanzania and Nairobi, Kenya kill approximately 212 people.

events.title: Mickey Leland
events.desc: U.S. Congressman Mickey Leland (D-TX) and 15 others die in a plane crash in Ethiopia.

events.title: Richard Nixon
events.desc: Richard Nixon appoints Luis R. Bruce, a Mohawk-Oglala Sioux and co-founder of the National Congress of American Indians, as the new commissioner of the Bureau of Indian Affairs.

events.title: IBM
events.desc: IBM dedicates the first program-controlled calculator, the Automatic Sequence Controlled Calculator (known best as the Harvard Mark I).

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Battle of Guadalcanal begins as the United States Marines initiate the first American offensive of the war with landings on Guadalcanal and Tulagi in the Solomon Islands.

events.title: Lynching
events.desc: The last confirmed lynching of black people in the Northern United States occurs in Marion, Indiana; two men, Thomas Shipp and Abram Smith, are murdered.

events.title: Alice Huyler Ramsey
events.desc: Alice Huyler Ramsey and three friends become the first women to complete a transcontinental auto trip, taking 59 days to travel from New York, New York to San Francisco, California.

events.title: Indian reservation
events.desc: The first federal Indian Reservation is created by the United States.

events.title: Ming dynasty
events.desc: The Ming dynasty Chinese military general Cao Qin stages a coup against the Tianshun Emperor.

births.name: David Duchovny
births.line: American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: Myint Swe, retired army general and acting president of Myanmar (b.1951)
Angus Tait, New Zealand businessman, founded Tait Communications
Jonathan P. Jackson, American bodyguard and kidnapper
Jöns Jacob Berzelius, Swedish chemist and academic
